assertion_results.text: The answer recommends `@pytest.mark.parametrize` for the repeated input cases
assertion_results.evidence: The grader did not return a result for this check.

assertion_results.text: The answer shows or describes a table of input and expected values in one focused test
assertion_results.evidence:
Provides a single test with a table of (email, expected):
"@pytest.mark.parametrize('email, expected', [("user@example.com", True), ...])\ndef test_is_valid_email_table(email, expected):\n    assert is_valid_email(email) == expected"

assertion_results.text: The answer avoids recommending many near-duplicate test functions for each case
assertion_results.evidence: Explicitly advises: "feed a table of inputs and expected outcomes into a single test function." The boolean-returning version uses one parametrized test; the exception-raising version uses two parametrized tests (valid vs. invalid), not many per-case functions.
